metadata.seed: My company has a holiday party and I just can't... go... I don't want to socialize and parties annoy me to no end. All the small talk, all the people... I don't want to go. At times I feel like I'm allergic to people.

patient.query: My company has a holiday party and I just can't... go... I don't want to socialize and parties annoy me to no end. All the small talk, all the people... I don't want to go. At times I feel like I'm allergic to people.

llm_response.response: It’s totally understandable to feel drained by big social gatherings—many people find the constant small talk and crowds exhausting. You don’t have to force yourself into a full‑blown party if it’s not right for you; you could consider a brief, low‑pressure visit (just a few minutes to say hello and grab a drink), or politely decline and let your manager know you’ll be out of the office for the event. If you do go, try to plan a “quiet corner” or a short break in a less crowded spot, and remember that setting boundaries is healthy—your well‑being matters more than the holiday cheer.
